heading: Exercise 1
text:
Look at the activities listed below. Reason out whether or
not work is done in the light of your understanding of the
term work .
Suma is swimming in a pond.
A donkey is carrying a load on its back.
A wind-mill is lifting water from a well.
A green plant is carrying out photosynthesis.
An engine is pulling a train.
Food grains are getting dried in the sun.
A sailboat is moving due to wind energy.

heading: Exercise 2
text:
An object thrown at a certain angle to the ground moves in
a curved path and falls back to the ground. The initial and
the final points of the path of the object lie on the same
horizontal line. What is the work done by the force of gravity
on the object

heading: Exercise 3
text:
A battery lights a bulb. Describe the energy changes
involved in the process.

heading: Exercise 4
text:
Certain force acting on a 20 kg mass changes its velocity
from 5 m s–1 to 2 m s–1. Calculate the work done by the
force.

heading: Exercise 5
text:
A mass of 10 kg is at a point A on a table. It is moved to a
point B. If the line joining A and B is horizontal, what is
the work done on the object by the gravitational force
Explain your answer.

heading: Exercise 6
text:
The potential energy of a freely falling object decreases
progressively. Does this violate the law of conservation of
energy Why

heading: Exercise 7
text:
What are the various energy transformations that occur
when you are riding a bicycle

heading: Exercise 8
text:
Does the transfer of energy take place when you push a
huge rock with all your might and fail to move it Where is
the energy you spend going

heading: Exercise 9
text:
A certain household has consumed 250 units of energy
during a month. How much energy is this in joules

heading: Exercise 10
text:
An object of mass 40 kg is raised to a height of 5 m above
the ground. What is its potential energy If the object is
allowed to fall, find its kinetic energy when it is half-way
down.

heading: Exercise 11
text:
What is the work done by the force of gravity on a satellite
moving round the earth Justify your answer.

heading: Exercise 12
text:
Can there be displacement of an object in the absence of
any force acting on it Think. Discuss this question with
your friends and teacher.

heading: Exercise 13
text:
A person holds a bundle of hay over his head for 30 minutes
and gets tired. Has he done some work or not Justify your
answer.

heading: Exercise 14
text:
An electric heater is rated 1500 W. How much energy does
it use in 10 hours

heading: Exercise 15
text:
Illustrate the law of conservation of energy by discussing
the energy changes which occur when we draw a pendulum
bob to one side and allow it to oscillate. Why does the bob

eventually come to rest What happens to its energy
eventually Is it a violation of the law of conservation of
energy

heading: Exercise 16
text:
An object of mass, m is moving with a constant velocity, v.
How much work should be done on the object in order to
bring the object to rest

heading: Exercise 17
text:
Calculate the work required to be done to stop a car of 1500
kg moving at a velocity of 60 km/h

heading: Exercise 18
text:
In each of the following a force, F is acting on an object of
mass, m. The direction of displacement is from west to east
shown by the longer arrow. Observe the diagrams carefully
and state whether the work done by the force is negative,
positive or zero.

heading: Exercise 19
text:
Soni says that the acceleration in an object could be zero
even when several forces are acting on it. Do you agree with
her Why

heading: Exercise 20
text:
Find the energy in joules consumed in 10 hours by four
devices of power 500 W each.

heading: Exercise 21
text:
A freely falling object eventually stops on reaching the
ground. What happenes to its kinetic energy
